what_it_does: Defines the constitution for the docs/ module: it is the repo's meta-documentation space for cross-cutting conventions and navigation, not for code or per-module documentation.
observations: Frontmatter declares type=constitution, register=prescriptive, module=docs, aliases, tags, relates-to links, and status=living.
The body uses structured sections: Is, Guarantees, Where new things go, To extend, Seam, and Never.
It explicitly states docs/ holds convention and navigation material, never code, and never duplicates a live source elsewhere.
It names [[Vault Conventions]] as the defining note for the dual repo+vault form every .md file obeys.
It references [[Company Map]], [[Company — read first]], and [[Coherence Gate — Spec]] but lists governs: [].
The Never section prohibits code, duplicate registries/specs, and convention drift.
standouts: The 'Never' section turns scope rules into explicit prohibitions: no code, no duplicate live sources, no drift.
It defines docs/ as cross-cutting meta-documentation, deliberately excluding per-module docs and code.
Status is marked 'living', signaling the note is expected to evolve with the repo.
conventions: Uses AGENTS.md as a module constitution file.
Follows frontmatter conventions with type, register, module, aliases, tags, relates-to, and status.
Separates Is, Guarantees, Where new things go, To extend, Seam, and Never as a repeatable constitution structure.
Declares a 'Seam' to make inbound/outbound references explicit.
Applies a prescriptive register and a living status.
notes: This is the constitution for the docs/ module, mainly setting boundaries: meta-documentation and conventions only, no code, no duplication, no drift.
questions: What specific conventions does [[Vault Conventions]] define?
What process or criteria does [[Coherence Gate — Spec]] use to detect drift?
How are 'per-module' docs distinguished from 'cross-cutting' docs in practice?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file presents itself as a constitution note for the docs/ module and does exactly that—defines scope, guarantees, extension rules, and prohibitions without containing code or module-specific documentation.
apparent_themes: Single source of truth and no duplication
intention_signals: It is trying to keep docs/ from becoming a dumping ground by strictly scoping it to cross-cutting meta-documentation, linking it into the company knowledge map, and delegating enforcement of coherence to a separate gate.
novelty: The explicit 'Seam' and 'Never' anti-rule sections, plus the 'living' status, give the constitution a boundary-oriented, self-policing shape rather than a plain description.
contribution: A clear, scoped definition of what belongs in docs/ and what does not, plus a reusable constitution structure with explicit guarantees, extension instructions, seams, and prohibitions.
summary_for_embedding: The docs/AGENTS.md constitution defines the docs module as the repo's meta-documentation space for cross-cutting conventions and navigation, forbidding code, duplicate live sources, and drift.
intention_for_embedding: To establish and enforce the boundary of the docs/ module as a home for repo-wide conventions that are linked into the company knowledge map and kept coherent with actual repo practice.
